pick.rationale: Arizona enters as a dominant favorite in this 1 vs 8 seed matchup, ranking #3 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite top-10 offense and top-3 defense that creates a roughly 18-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Villanova's 33rd-36th ranked profile. The Wildcats appear to have their key freshman forward Koa Peat available after his return for the Big 12 Tournament, while Villanova is dealing with a significant blow from losing starting wing Matt Hodge to a season-ending ACL injury for all of March Madness. Historical 1-seed success rates in the Round of 32 combined with Arizona's national championship contender status and Villanova's depleted roster make the Wildcats the clear choice in this West Region matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs Villanova Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona holds a massive quality advantage as a #3 nationally ranked team facing a Villanova squad missing a key starter to injury, creating a clear favorite scenario in this 1 vs 8 seed matchup.
reasoningStep.evidence: Arizona ranks #3 in KenPom/Torvik with +37.56 adj EM vs Villanova's 33rd-36th ranking with +19.94 adj EM (18-point gap). Villanova lost starting wing Matt Hodge to season-ending ACL injury. Arizona's Koa Peat returned for Big 12 Tournament and appears available. Historical 1-seed Round of 32 win rate exceeds 80%.

By Alan Rubenstein | Share on Facebook Share on X Share on Reddit Copy Article Add us as a preferred source Arizona Wildcats forward Koa Peat (10) awaits for an inbound pass during the game against Kansas Jayhawks inside Allen Fieldhouse on Feb. 9, 2026. | Evert Nelson/The Capital-Journal / USA TODAY NETWORK via Imagn Images Arizona was without freshmen forwards Dwayne Aristode for the entire game and Koa Peat for the second half in the loss to Texas Tech on Saturday. Aristode was out with an illness and Peat sat out the second half with a leg injury, according to Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd in the post game. --> Arizona was without freshmen forwards Dwayne Aristode for the entire game and Koa Peat for the second half in the loss to Texas Tech on Saturday. Aristode was out with an illness and Peat sat out the second half with a leg injury, according to Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd in the post game. Peat had consecutive games below 10 points for the first time this season. Playing 11 minutes, Peat scored two points without taking a shot from the field and had one rebound as the game was tied at 32 at halftime. Peat has a lower leg injury that Arizona trainer Justin Kokoskie and doctors will examine. --> Peat had consecutive games below 10 points for the first time this season. Peat has a lower leg injury that Arizona trainer Justin Kokoskie and doctors will examine. Aristode has an undisclosed illness that Lloyd described as " nothing crazy," but that will keep him out for a short while. When further asked if Aristode will play against BYU on Wednesday, Lloyd replied, "I don't know for sure...but I think there's a chance." --> Aristode has an undisclosed illness that Lloyd described as " nothing crazy," but that will keep him out for a short while. When further asked if Aristode will play against BYU on Wednesday, Lloyd replied, "I don't know for sure...but I think there's a chance." Senior forward Tobe Awaka started in place of Peat in the second half. Lloyd played seven players in the first half and six in the second without Peat. You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

This suggests he recovered from his earlier injury and was available for the tournament. The article from AP News mentions him playing in the Big 12 championship, which would be just before the NCAA Tournament.

So it appears:
- Koa Peat (Arizona) - Was injured in February but appears to have returned for the Big 12 Tournament and should be available for March Madness
- Matt Hodge (Villanova) - Has a season-ending ACL injury and will miss all of March Madness

This is significant. Villanova is missing a starting wing, while Arizona appears to be at or near full strength.

Let me now compile my analysis:

Arizona:
- Ranked #3 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik
- 31-2 record (now 32-2 after Round of 64)
- Top-10 offense, top-3 defense
- +37.56 adjusted efficiency margin (KenPom)
- Koa Peat appears to have returned from injury for the tournament
- #1 seed with overwhelming favorite status in Round of 64 (94% confidence pick)

Villanova:
- Ranked 33rd-36th nationally
- 24-8 record
- Solid but not elite profile (top-45 offense and defense)
- +19.94 adjusted efficiency margin (KenPom)
- Missing starting wing Matt Hodge to season-ending ACL injury
- #8 seed who narrowly won their Round of 64 game (57% confidence pick)

The efficiency gap is substantial (~17-18 points). Arizona is a national championship contender while Villanova is a solid but not elite team, now dealing with a key injury.

Historical 1 vs 8 seed matchups in the Round of 32 heavily favor the 1-seed. The combination of Arizona's elite profile, Villanova's injury, and the massive efficiency gap makes Arizona a very strong favorite.

However, I should be careful not to be overconfident. Villanova is a Big East team with tournament experience, and 8-seeds do occasionally pull upsets. But the injury to Matt Hodge significantly reduces their chances.

My confidence should be high but not extreme - maybe around 80-85%. Let me finalize my pick.

- Arizona is a #1 seed ranked #3 nationally with elite offense and defense

- Villanova is missing starting wing Matt Hodge to a season-ending ACL injury
- Arizona's Koa Peat appears to have returned from his earlier injury and played in the Big 12 Tournament
- Historical 1 vs 8 seed matchups in Round of 32 heavily favor the 1-seed

This is a clear favorite situation. I'll pick Arizona with high confidence (around 82-85%).
